server.c(3) Library Functions Manual server.c(3)

NAME

server.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *server, const acds_config_t *config)
Initialize discovery server. asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *server)
Run discovery server main loop. void acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t *server)
Shutdown discovery server. void * acds_client_handler (void *arg)
Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). void * acds_websocket_client_handler (void *arg)
WebSocket client handler (thread entry point).

Function Documentation

void * acds_client_handler (void * arg)

Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP packets from a connected client. Handles crypto handshake, then dispatches packets to session/signaling handlers.

Parameters

arg Pointer to client connection context

Returns

NULL (thread exit value)

Definition at line 859 of file src/discovery-service/server.c.

859                                      {
860   tcp_client_context_t *ctx = (tcp_client_context_t *)arg;
861   if (!ctx) {
862     log_error("Client handler: NULL context");
863     return NULL;
864   }
865 
866   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
867   socket_t client_socket = ctx->client_socket;
868 
869   // Get client IP for logging
870   char client_ip[INET6_ADDRSTRLEN] = {0};
871   tcp_client_context_get_ip(ctx, client_ip, sizeof(client_ip));
872 
873   log_info("Client handler started for %s", client_ip);
874 
875   // Register client in TCP server registry with allocated client data
876   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
877   if (!client_data) {
878     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to allocate client data");
879     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
880     return NULL;
881   }
882   memset(client_data, 0, sizeof(*client_data));
883   client_data->joined_session = false;
884   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
885 
886   // Initialize crypto handshake context with client IP name
887   char crypto_name[96];
888   SAFE_SNPRINTF(crypto_name, sizeof(crypto_name), "crypto_discovery_client_%s", client_ip);
889   asciichat_error_t handshake_result = crypto_handshake_init(crypto_name, &client_data->handshake_ctx, true);
890   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
891     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto handshake for client %s", client_ip);
892     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
893     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
894     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
895     return NULL;
896   }
897 
898   // Set server identity keys for handshake
899   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
900   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
901   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.key, server->identity_public, 32);
902   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.key.ed25519, server->identity_secret, 64);
903 
904   if (tcp_server_add_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
905     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
906     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to register client in registry");
907     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
908     return NULL;
909   }
910 
911   log_debug("Client %s registered (socket=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, client_socket,
912             tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
913 
914   // Create transport for handshake and subsequent client communication
915   acip_transport_t *transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("acds_tcp_client", client_socket, NULL);
916   if (!transport) {
917     log_error("Failed to create TCP transport for client %s", client_ip);
918     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
919     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
920     return NULL;
921   }
922 
923   // Perform crypto handshake (three-step process)
924   log_debug("Performing crypto handshake with client %s", client_ip);
925 
926   // Step 0: Send crypto parameters
927   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport);
928   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
929     log_warn("Crypto parameters send failed for client %s", client_ip);
930     acip_transport_destroy(transport);
931     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
932     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
933     return NULL;
934   }
935 
936   // Step 1: Start handshake (send server key, receive client key)
937   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_start(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport);
938   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
939     log_warn("Crypto handshake start failed for client %s", client_ip);
940     acip_transport_destroy(transport);
941     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
942     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
943     return NULL;
944   }
945 
946   // Step 2: Authentication challenge (if required)
947   {
948     packet_type_t packet_type;
949     uint8_t *payload = NULL;
950     size_t payload_len = 0;
951     int recv_result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
952     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
953       log_warn("Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE from client %s", client_ip);
954       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
955       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
956       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
957       return NULL;
958     }
959     handshake_result =
960         crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
961     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
962   }
963   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
964     log_warn("Crypto handshake auth challenge failed for client %s", client_ip);
965     acip_transport_destroy(transport);
966     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
967     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
968     return NULL;
969   }
970 
971   // Step 3: Complete handshake (verify and finalize) - skip if already complete
972   if (client_data->handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
973     packet_type_t packet_type = 0;
974     uint8_t *payload = NULL;
975     size_t payload_len = 0;
976     int recv_result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
977     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
978       log_warn("Failed to receive AUTH_RESPONSE from client %s", client_ip);
979       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
980       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
981       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
982       return NULL;
983     }
984     handshake_result =
985         crypto_handshake_server_complete(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
986     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
987     if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
988       log_warn("Crypto handshake complete failed for client %s", client_ip);
989       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
990       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
991       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
992       return NULL;
993     }
994   } else {
995     log_debug("Crypto handshake already complete from auth_challenge step for client %s", client_ip);
996   }
997 
998   client_data->handshake_complete = true;
999   log_info("Crypto handshake complete for client %s", client_ip);
1000 
1001   // Store transport and registry ID in client data, and link back from transport
1002   client_data->transport = transport;
1003   client_data->registry_id = client_socket;
1004   transport->user_data = client_data;
1005 
1006   // Main packet processing loop
1007   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server.running)) {
1008     packet_type_t packet_type;
1009     void *payload = NULL;
1010     size_t payload_size = 0;
1011 
1012     // Receive packet (blocking with system timeout)
1013     int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_size);
1014     if (result < 0) {
1015       // Check error context to distinguish timeout from actual disconnect
1016       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1017       bool has_context = HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx);
1018 
1019       // Check if this is a timeout (non-fatal) or actual disconnect (fatal)
1020       asciichat_error_t error = GET_ERRNO();
1021       if (error == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT ||
1022           (error == ERROR_NETWORK && has_context && strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "timed out") != NULL)) {
1023         // Timeout waiting for next packet - this is normal, continue waiting
1024         log_debug("Client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
1025         if (payload) {
1026           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1027         }
1028         continue;
1029       }
1030 
1031       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
1032       log_info("Client %s disconnected", client_ip);
1033       if (payload) {
1034         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1035       }
1036       break;
1037     }
1038 
1039     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1040 
1041     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
1042     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
1043       bool allowed =
1044           (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
1045 
1046       if (!allowed) {
1047         log_warn("Client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG "
1048                  "allowed",
1049                  client_ip, packet_type);
1050 
1051         // Send error response using persistent transport
1052         acip_send_error(transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1053                         "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
1054 
1055         // Free payload and continue
1056         if (payload) {
1057           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1058         }
1059         continue;
1060       }
1061     }
1062 
1063     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
1064     // Set server context for callbacks
1065     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
1066     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
1067 
1068     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
1069         acip_handle_acds_packet(transport, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_ip, &callbacks);
1070 
1071     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1072       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
1073                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1074     }
1075 
1076     // Free payload (allocated by receive_packet via buffer_pool_alloc)
1077     if (payload) {
1078       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1079     }
1080   }
1081 
1082   // Cleanup
1083   tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1084   log_debug("Client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip, tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1085 
1086   // Destroy transport before closing socket
1087   acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1088 
1089   socket_close(client_socket);
1090   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1091 
1092   log_info("Client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1093   return NULL;
1094 }




References acip_handle_acds_packet(),
    acip_send_error(), acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(), acip_acds_callbacks_t::app_ctx,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(), buffer_pool_free(),
    tcp_client_context_t::client_socket,
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    crypto_handshake_init(), CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(),
    crypto_handshake_server_complete(),
    crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters(),
    crypto_handshake_server_start(), private_key_t::ed25519,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK,
    ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, GET_ERRNO,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_complete,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_ctx, HAS_ERRNO,
    acds_server_t::identity_public,
    acds_server_t::identity_secret,
    acds_client_data_t::in_multikey_session_create,
    acds_client_data_t::joined_session, private_key_t::key,
    public_key_t::key, KEY_TYPE_ED25519, log_debug,

    acds_client_data_t::registry_id, tcp_server::running,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_SNPRINTF,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_private_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_public_key, socket_close(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state,
    tcp_client_context_get_ip(), acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_add_client(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    tcp_server_reject_client(), tcp_server_remove_client(),
    acds_client_data_t::transport, private_key_t::type,
    public_key_t::type, acip_transport::user_data, and
    tcp_client_context_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_server_init().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *
  server, const acds_config_t * config)


Initialize discovery server. Loads or generates identity keys,
    opens database, creates session registry, and binds TCP socket.




Parameters


server Server structure to initialize


Config Module Configuration from command-line
  parsing


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 191 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


191                                                                                        {
192   if (!server || !config) {
193     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server or config is NULL");
194   }
195 
196   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
197   memcpy(&server->config, config, sizeof(acds_config_t));
198 
199   // Open database (SQLite as single source of truth)
200   asciichat_error_t result = database_init(config->database_path, &server->db);
201   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
202     return result;
203   }
204 
205   // Initialize rate limiter with SQLite backend
206   server->rate_limiter = rate_limiter_create_sqlite(NULL); // NULL = externally managed DB
207   if (!server->rate_limiter) {
208     database_close(server->db);
209     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create rate limiter");
210   }
211 
212   // Set the database handle for the rate limiter
213   rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(server->rate_limiter, server->db);
214 
215   // Configure TCP server
216   tcp_server_config_t tcp_config = {
217       .port = config->port,
218       .ipv4_address = (config->address[0] != '\0') ? config->address : NULL,
219       .ipv6_address = (config->address6[0] != '\0') ? config->address6 : NULL,
220       .bind_ipv4 = (config->address[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
221       .bind_ipv6 = (config->address6[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
222       .accept_timeout_sec = 1,
223       .client_handler = acds_client_handler,
224       .user_data = server,
225   };
226 
227   // Initialize TCP server
228   result = tcp_server_init(&server->tcp_server, &tcp_config);
229   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
230     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
231     database_close(server->db);
232     return result;
233   }
234 
235   // Initialize background worker thread pool
236   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, false);
237   server->worker_pool = thread_pool_create("acds_workers");
238   if (!server->worker_pool) {
239     log_warn("Failed to create worker thread pool");
240     tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
241     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
242     database_close(server->db);
243     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create worker thread pool");
244   }
245 
246   // Spawn cleanup thread in worker pool
247   if (thread_pool_spawn(server->worker_pool, cleanup_thread_func, server, 0, "cleanup") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
248     log_warn("Failed to spawn cleanup thread (continuing without cleanup)");
249   }
250 
251   log_info("Discovery server initialized successfully");
252   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
253 }

    acds_config_t::address, acds_config_t::address6,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_store_bool(),
    acds_server_t::config, database_close(),
    database_init(), acds_config_t::database_path,
    acds_server_t::db, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    log_info, log_warn, acds_config_t::port,
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_create_sqlite(),
    rate_limiter_destroy(), rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(),
    SET_ERRNO, acds_server_t::shutdown,
    acds_server_t::tcp_server, tcp_server_destroy(),
    tcp_server_init(), thread_pool_create(),
    thread_pool_spawn(), and acds_server_t::worker_pool.


Referenced by acds_main().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *
  server)


Run discovery server main loop. Accepts client connections and
    spawns handler threads. Blocks until shutdown signal received.




Parameters


server Initialized server structure


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 255 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


255                                                          {
256   if (!server) {
257     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server is NULL");
258   }
259 
260   log_info("Discovery server accepting connections on port %d", server->config.port);
261 
262   // Delegate to TCP server abstraction
263   return tcp_server_run(&server->tcp_server);
264 }






References acds_server_t::config,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_info, acds_config_t::port,
    SET_ERRNO, acds_server_t::tcp_server, and
    tcp_server_run().


Referenced by acds_main().






void
  acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t * server)


Shutdown discovery server. Closes listen socket, stops accepting
    connections, waits for handler threads to exit, closes database, and frees
    resources.




Parameters


server Server structure to clean up


Definition at line 266 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


266                                                  {
267   if (!server) {
268     return;
269   }
270 
271   // Signal shutdown to worker threads
272   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, true);
273 
274   // Shutdown TCP server (closes listen sockets, stops accept loop)
275   tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
276 
277   // Wait for all client handler threads to exit
278   size_t remaining_clients;
279   int shutdown_attempts = 0;
280   const int max_shutdown_attempts = 100; // 10 seconds (100 * 100ms)
281 
282   while ((remaining_clients = tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server)) > 0 &&
283          shutdown_attempts < max_shutdown_attempts) {
284     log_debug("Waiting for %zu client handler threads to exit (attempt %d/%d)", remaining_clients,
285               shutdown_attempts + 1, max_shutdown_attempts);
286     platform_sleep_ms(100);
287     shutdown_attempts++;
288   }
289 
290   if (remaining_clients > 0) {
291     log_warn("Server shutdown: %zu client handler threads still running after 10 seconds", remaining_clients);
292   } else if (shutdown_attempts > 0) {
293     log_debug("All client handler threads exited gracefully");
294   }
295 
296   // Stop and destroy worker thread pool (cleanup thread, etc.)
297   if (server->worker_pool) {
298     thread_pool_destroy(server->worker_pool);
299     server->worker_pool = NULL;
300     log_debug("Worker thread pool stopped");
301   }
302 
303   // Destroy rate limiter
304   if (server->rate_limiter) {
305     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
306     server->rate_limiter = NULL;
307   }
308 
309   // Close database
310   if (server->db) {
311     database_close(server->db);
312     server->db = NULL;
313   }
314 
315   log_info("Server shutdown complete");
316 }






References atomic_store_bool(), database_close(),
    acds_server_t::db, log_debug, log_info,
    log_warn, platform_sleep_ms(),
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_destroy(),
    acds_server_t::shutdown, acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_destroy(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    thread_pool_destroy(), and acds_server_t::worker_pool.


Referenced by acds_main().






void
  * acds_websocket_client_handler (void * arg)


WebSocket client handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP
    packets from a WebSocket client connection. Similar to TCP handler but uses
    transport-based APIs for crypto handshake.




Parameters


arg Pointer to
  websocket_client_context_t


Returns


NULL (thread exit value)


Definition at line 1100 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


1100                                                {
1101   websocket_client_context_t *ctx = (websocket_client_context_t *)arg;
1102   if (!ctx) {
1103     log_error("WebSocket client handler: NULL context");
1104     return NULL;
1105   }
1106 
1107   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
1108   acip_transport_t *transport = ctx->transport;
1109   char *client_ip = ctx->client_ip;
1110 
1111   log_info("WebSocket client handler started for %s", client_ip);
1112 
1113   // Allocate client_data
1114   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
1115   if (!client_data) {
1116     log_error("Failed to allocate client data for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1117     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1118     return NULL;
1119   }
1120 
1121   client_data->transport = transport;
1122   client_data->joined_session = false;
1123   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
1124   transport->user_data = client_data;
1125 
1126   // Generate synthetic socket ID for tcp_server registry
1127   static _Atomic int g_ws_synthetic_id = INT_MAX;
1128   int synthetic_id = atomic_fetch_sub(&g_ws_synthetic_id, 1);
1129   client_data->registry_id = synthetic_id;
1130 
1131   // Register in tcp_server with synthetic ID (so callbacks can find client_data)
1132   if (tcp_server_add_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1133     log_error("Failed to register WebSocket client %s in registry", client_ip);
1134     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
1135     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1136     return NULL;
1137   }
1138 
1139   log_debug("WebSocket client %s registered (synthetic_id=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, synthetic_id,
1140             tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1141 
1142   // For WebSocket with TLS (wss://), skip crypto handshake since TLS already provides encryption
1143   // For plain ws://, we should still do crypto handshake, but for now we skip it
1144   // TODO: Add crypto handshake support for plain ws:// connections
1145   client_data->handshake_complete = true;
1146   log_info("WebSocket connection from %s - skipping crypto handshake (TLS encryption assumed)", client_ip);
1147 
1148   // Main packet processing loop using transport recv
1149   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server.running)) {
1150     void *recv_buffer = NULL;
1151     size_t recv_len = 0;
1152     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1153 
1154     asciichat_error_t recv_result = acip_transport_recv(transport, &recv_buffer, &recv_len, &alloc_buffer);
1155     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1156       // Check if this is a timeout or disconnect
1157       if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1158         log_debug("WebSocket client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
1159         if (alloc_buffer) {
1160           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1161         }
1162         continue;
1163       }
1164 
1165       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
1166       log_info("WebSocket client %s disconnected", client_ip);
1167       if (alloc_buffer) {
1168         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1169       }
1170       break;
1171     }
1172 
1173     // Parse packet header from received data
1174     if (recv_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1175       log_warn("WebSocket client %s: received packet too small (%zu bytes)", client_ip, recv_len);
1176       if (alloc_buffer) {
1177         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1178       }
1179       continue;
1180     }
1181 
1182     const packet_header_t *header = (const packet_header_t *)recv_buffer;
1183     packet_type_t packet_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
1184     size_t payload_size = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->length);
1185     const void *payload = (const uint8_t *)recv_buffer + sizeof(packet_header_t);
1186 
1187     // Validate payload size
1188     if (recv_len < sizeof(packet_header_t) + payload_size) {
1189       log_warn("WebSocket client %s: packet size mismatch (expected %zu, got %zu)", client_ip,
1190                sizeof(packet_header_t) + payload_size, recv_len);
1191       if (alloc_buffer) {
1192         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1193       }
1194       continue;
1195     }
1196 
1197     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from WebSocket client %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1198 
1199     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
1200     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
1201       bool allowed =
1202           (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
1203 
1204       if (!allowed) {
1205         log_warn("WebSocket client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only "
1206                  "SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed",
1207                  client_ip, packet_type);
1208         acip_send_error(transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1209                         "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
1210         if (alloc_buffer) {
1211           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1212         }
1213         continue;
1214       }
1215     }
1216 
1217     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
1218     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
1219     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
1220 
1221     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
1222         acip_handle_acds_packet(transport, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_ip, &callbacks);
1223 
1224     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1225       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from WebSocket client %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
1226                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1227     }
1228 
1229     if (alloc_buffer) {
1230       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1231     }
1232   }
1233 
1234   // Cleanup
1235   tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1236   log_debug("WebSocket client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip, tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1237 
1238   // Transport is owned by WebSocket server, don't destroy it here
1239 
1240   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1241 
1242   log_info("WebSocket client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1243   return NULL;
1244 }






References acip_handle_acds_packet(),
    acip_send_error(), acip_acds_callbacks_t::app_ctx,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(), buffer_pool_free(),
    websocket_client_context_t::client_ip, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, acds_client_data_t::handshake_complete,
    acds_client_data_t::in_multikey_session_create,
    acds_client_data_t::joined_session, packet_header_t::length,
    log_debug, log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    NET_TO_HOST_U16, NET_TO_HOST_U32,
    PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE, PACKET_TYPE_PING,
    PACKET_TYPE_PONG, acds_client_data_t::registry_id,
    tcp_server::running, SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    acds_server_t::tcp_server, tcp_server_add_client(),
    tcp_server_get_client_count(), tcp_server_remove_client(),
    acds_client_data_t::transport,
    websocket_client_context_t::transport, packet_header_t::type,
    acip_transport::user_data, and
    websocket_client_context_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_main().

    ascii-chat
